title: Acceptance Agent verdict
description: Output shape for the Acceptance Agent's milestone-close / release-cut / sub-sprint judgment. Per Constitution §3.5: on milestone_verdict: fix_required, the Acceptance Agent ALSO writes a human-confirm checkpoint file (process/delivery-loop.md §4.2.3 item 'acceptance_fix_required'); Deliver picks up the gap brief ONLY after that checkpoint resolves with confirm: yes + route: deliver_fix_iteration. P-C: acceptance_class ∈ {static (M1, absent⇒static), browser_e2e (M3)} selects the per-case branch — static requires evidence_path (^eval/runs/.+); browser_e2e requires criterion_id + functional_evidence_refs (under .orchestrator/audit/browser/). The DRIVER additionally enforces active-class match and binds each functional_evidence_ref to the committed browser-evidence-manifest (a fake/uncommitted ref → gate_hard_fail).

acceptance_class.description: P-C. Absent ⇒ static (M1, today's behavior, byte-identical). browser_e2e (M3) selects the functional branch. No schema default (so absence stays distinguishable); the driver rejects a browser_e2e RUN whose verdict is not acceptance_class: browser_e2e.

authoritative.description: P-C. DERIVED, never self-asserted: the driver stamps whether the verdict was authoritative at production (mode==auto AND calibrated-for-active-class AND fully_autonomous). Advisory (false/absent) HALTs for human sign-off.

calibration_record_id.description: P-C. The acceptance-calibration-record id (per active class M1/M3) matched at verdict time; absent when uncalibrated / not_required.

closure_contract_ref.description: Path to docs/research-briefs/<id>.md whose closure_contract was the evaluation source. Front-matter sign_off_date should match milestone start (Constitution §3.4 invariant #4 — Research-Acceptance contract symmetry).

calibration_status.description: Confirms §3.6 calibration gate state at verdict time (for the ACTIVE class). 'not_required' means autonomy.level was human_in_the_loop or human_on_the_loop.

case_id.description: closure_contract clause ref OR bad-case suite id.

criterion_id.description: P-C (browser_e2e). The functional-checklist criterion_id this case judges. Required + unique in the browser_e2e branch; the driver checks set-equality coverage against the signed functional-checklist.

criterion.description: The specific clause text or summary being judged.

evidence_path.description: Static (M1) F5 execution-evidence artifact path. REQUIRED + patterned ^eval/runs/.+ in the static branch (below); omitted for browser_e2e (which cites functional_evidence_refs instead). Acceptance verdicts citing code paths instead of execution evidence violate §4.2.8 anti-pattern #5.

functional_evidence_refs.description: P-C (browser_e2e). Citations into the COMMITTED browser-evidence-manifest. Required + constrained in the browser branch (below). The driver binds every {path,sha256} to a committed manifest artifact (mismatch / uncommitted → gate_hard_fail).

rationale.description: Paragraph citing positive shape held, anti-pattern avoided, anchor-phrase semantic match (NOT keyword match per Constitution §1.7-B). For browser_e2e, cite the specific observed artifact; executor_status values in the evidence are OBSERVATIONS, not verdicts.

proposed_scope.description: Paragraph describing what Deliver should fix if confirm: yes + route: deliver_fix_iteration.

failure_briefs.description: Required (non-empty) iff milestone_verdict = fix_required (enforced by the conditional below).

residual_risks.description: Optional; residual risks Customer assumes if milestone_verdict = pass.

suggested_route.description: n/a applies when milestone_verdict = pass. For fix_required: pick the route that best fits the failure shape per role-cards/acceptance-agent.md §7.1.

human_confirm_checkpoint_path.description: Required if milestone_verdict = fix_required: path to docs/checkpoints/<timestamp>__acceptance_fix_required__<scope>.md written by Acceptance per Constitution §3.5. Verdict-without-checkpoint is a §1.7-C breach.

allOf.$comment: P-C branch-correct cases: JSON-Schema constraints are additive, so evidence_path is NOT in the base cases.items.required — it is re-required (with the ^eval/runs/.+ pattern) only in the static ELSE branch; browser_e2e requires criterion_id + functional_evidence_refs. A static verdict (no acceptance_class) takes ELSE and is byte-identical to the pre-P-C contract.
fix_required ⇒ non-empty failure_briefs (Constitution §3.5; the driver also enforces pass⇒empty + cross-consistency).
